payload.answer: Under Admin SQL select-only mode, read-only queries are allowed (e.g., `SELECT id FROM memory_cards LIMIT 10`). Any write operation like `DELETE FROM memory_cards` is denied, so there is no conflict for the SELECT case but a policy conflict if a DELETE is attempted.
payload.conflict_classification: Policy enforcement conflict: operation violates select-only restriction (DELETE denied).
payload.decision: No conflict for SELECT; conflict for DELETE (denied).

entity_resolution.reason: No new entities or memory facts were provided beyond the stated policy constraints.

payload.intent: Explain a SQL policy conflict under Admin SQL select-only mode and determine whether the requested SQL operation is allowed.

details: Confirmed select-only allowance for querying `memory_cards` (SELECT allowed).
Confirmed write denial for deleting from `memory_cards` (DELETE denied).

repair_options: Use a SELECT query only (e.g., `SELECT id FROM memory_cards LIMIT 10`).
If deletion is required, route the request to a mode/permission that allows write operations (not available in select-only mode).
For “removal” behavior, implement it at the application layer without issuing SQL DELETE (if permitted by the system design).
